records.ety: [OE. iniquitee, F. iniquit, L. iniquitas, inequality, unfairness, injustice. See Iniquous.]

defs: Absence of, or deviation from, just dealing; lack of rectitude or uprightness; gross injustice; unrighteousness; wickedness; as, the iniquity of bribery; the iniquity of an unjust judge.
quotes: Till the world from his perfection fell Into all filth and foul iniquity.

defs: An iniquitous act or thing; a deed of injustice or unrighteousness; a sin; a crime.
quotes: Your iniquities have separated between you and your God.

defs: A character or personification in the old English moralities, or moral dramas, having the name sometimes of one vice and sometimes of another. See Vice.
quotes: Acts old Iniquity, and in the fit Of miming gets the opinion of a wit.
